intro: The clearest split is that React Native and Capacitor overlap on scope but take different approaches. React Native is Release / App Store Operations tool for developers. Specializes in Cross Platform Framework; Capacitor is Release / App Store Operations tool for developers. Specializes in Cross Platform Framework. The data points React Native at mobile app release automation and over-the-air updates, and Capacitor at mobile app release automation and over-the-air updates. Pricing: React Native is free, free tier; Capacitor is free, free tier. Lock-in is low for React Native and low for Capacitor, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, React Native emphasises automates app store submissions and release management, and Capacitor emphasises mobile app release automation and over-the-air updates. Neither lists an enterprise tier, so contract-level buying may require a direct conversation. Honest trade-off: React Native is not for web-only products without native app distribution; Capacitor is not for internal projects, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: React Native is for mobile teams shipping frequent app updates; Capacitor is for mobile; decide based on which scope-fit aligns with your team.
verdict_a: Choose React Native if your workload matches mobile teams shipping frequent app updates and your work involves mobile app release automation. Skip it if your context is web-only products without native app distribution.
verdict_b: Choose Capacitor if your workload matches mobile and your work involves mobile app release automation. Skip it if your context is internal projects.

faq.q: Is React Native cheaper than Capacitor?
faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for React Native and unknown for Capacitor. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from React Native to Capacitor?
faq.a: React Native is tagged with low lock-in (oss cross-platform framework). Capacitor is tagged with low lock-in (open-source cross-platform framework). Both sides are low lock-in, so a migration is feasible but still needs planning around data export and SDK rewrites.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, React Native or Capacitor?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Capacitor a good alternative to React Native?
faq.a: Capacitor is listed as fitting mobile, which overlaps with but is not identical to React Native's scope. Capacitor is flagged as not for internal projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
